steps: Go to https://dashboard.algolia.com/ and sign in (or create a free account)
In the left sidebar, click 'Settings' (gear icon)
Your Application ID, Admin API Key, and Search-Only API Key are listed here
To create a restricted API key, click 'New API Key', configure permissions and index restrictions, then click 'Create'
Copy both your Application ID and the desired API key — both are required for every request

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: Your Algolia API key (Admin or restricted). Sent in the X-Algolia-API-Key header.

notes: Algolia requires TWO credentials per request: API key (X-Algolia-API-Key) AND Application ID (X-Algolia-Application-Id)
Application ID is not secret — it identifies your Algolia application
Admin API Key has full write access — use a restricted key in client-side code
Free plan: 10,000 records and 10,000 search requests/month
The base URL is application-specific: https://{APPLICATION_ID}.algolia.net or https://{APPLICATION_ID}-dsn.algolia.net
Store your Application ID alongside the API key when configuring this integration

test_endpoint.description: List all indices in the application (requires Admin API key and Application ID headers)
